home.description: A community-driven database for electronic components, schematics, and repair documentation.
home.subscription_confirmed: SUBSCRIPTION_CONFIRMED — You'll be notified at launch.
home.building_subtitle: // A repair knowledge base for everyone
home.feature1_desc: Cross-reference ICs, transistors, and parts across thousands of consumer electronics.
home.feature2_desc: Service manuals, block diagrams, and bills of materials from teardowns.
home.feature3_desc: Built by repair enthusiasts, for repair enthusiasts. Open data for everyone.
home.early_access_desc: We're looking for beta testers and early contributors to help build the database.
home.r2r_desc: Manufacturers don't want you to fix your devices. We're building a database to help you fix it anyway. Every documented component, every shared schematic, every teardown photo helps independent repair shops, hobbyists, and consumers fight for their right to repair.
home.learn_r2r: LEARN MORE ABOUT RIGHT TO REPAIR →

guidelines.title: COMMUNITY GUIDELINES
guidelines.subtitle: Standards for contributing to the Junkbin.io community
guidelines.mission_text: Junkbin.io is a community-driven database for electronic component documentation, supporting the Right to Repair movement. We believe repair information should be freely accessible. Every contribution helps someone fix a device instead of throwing it away. These guidelines exist to keep the community productive, respectful, and focused on that goal.
guidelines.content_01_text: Double-check part numbers, manufacturer names, and reference designators before submitting. If you're unsure, note it in the description.
guidelines.content_02_text: When uploading schematics or datasheets, indicate where they came from (manufacturer site, community reverse-engineering, etc.).
guidelines.content_03_text: Search before submitting a new product or component. If it already exists, add to the existing entry instead.
guidelines.content_04_text: When uploading PCB images, use good lighting and focus. Clear images help others identify components.
guidelines.content_05_text: Comments should relate to the product, its components, or repair information. Save off-topic discussions for messages.
guidelines.comm_01_text: Disagreements about component identification are normal. Discuss the data, not the person.
guidelines.comm_02_text: Targeted, repeated, or unwelcome messages to another user are not tolerated. Use the block feature if needed.
guidelines.comm_03_text: Slurs, discriminatory language, and content promoting hatred against any group are prohibited.
guidelines.comm_04_text: Don't send unsolicited promotional messages or post repetitive content.
guidelines.prohibited_intro: The following will result in content removal and may lead to account action:
guidelines.prohibited_03: Copyright-infringing material without fair use basis
guidelines.prohibited_06: Hate speech, slurs, or discriminatory language
guidelines.prohibited_07: Impersonation of other users or organizations
guidelines.enforcement_intro: We use a transparent, graduated enforcement system:
guidelines.enforcement_report_text: Any user can report content or messages that violate these guidelines. Reports are reviewed by moderators.
guidelines.enforcement_strike_text: Valid reports result in a strike on the reported user's account. You'll be notified by email when you receive a strike.
guidelines.enforcement_review_text: At 3 strikes, your account is automatically flagged for moderator review. Reviews can result in:
guidelines.outcome_cleared_text: No action needed, strikes were minor or context-dependent
guidelines.outcome_warning_text: Formal warning issued, no account restrictions
guidelines.outcome_restricted_text: Trusted status revoked, contributions require moderation
guidelines.appeals_text_before: If you believe a moderation action was made in error, you can appeal by emailing
guidelines.appeals_text_after: with your username and a description of the situation. Appeals are reviewed by a different moderator than the one who took the original action. We aim to respond within 48 hours.
